title: Microfluidic device
abstract: Disclosed is a microfluidic device for detecting the presence and/or amount of analytes in a fluid comprising a first and a second element both comprising a hydrophobic surface. Both hydrophobic surfaces comprise a hydrophilic pathway, wherein the hydrophilic pathways are configured in a mirror image of each other in such a way that when the first and second elements are placed upon each other with the hydrophilic pathways facing each other a capillary fluid channel on the hydrophobic surfaces of the elements is formed, and wherein at least one of the hydrophilic pathways comprises a sensing region comprising capture means.
claims:
Claims 1 . A microfluidic device for detecting the presence and/or amount of an analyte in a fluid, comprising a first and a second element (1 a, 1 b) both comprising a hydrophobic surface (2a, 2b), wherein the hydrophobic surfaces each comprise a hydrophilic pathway (3a, 3b), wherein the hydrophilic pathways are configured in a mirror image of each other in such a way that they form a capillary fluid channel (4) when the first and second elements are placed upon each other with the hydrophilic pathways facing each other, and wherein at least one of the hydrophilic pathways comprises a sensing region (5a, 5b) comprising capture means (6a, 6b). 2. The microfluidic device of claim 1 , wherein the first and the second element (1 a, 1 b) comprise a hydrophilic pathway (3a, 3b) comprising a sensing region (5a, 5b) comprising capture means (6a, 6b). 3. The microfluidic device of claim 1 or 2, wherein the first and second element (1 a, 1 b) each comprise two or more hydrophilic pathways comprising a sensing region comprising capture means. 4. The microfluidic device of any one of claims 1 -3, wherein when the first and second element (1 a, 1 b) are placed upon each other, an air layer (7) between the hydrophobic surfaces aligns the lateral sides of the hydrophilic pathways, thereby laterally delimiting the capillary fluid channel. 5. The microfluidic device of any one of claims 1 -4, wherein the distance between the first and second element (1 a, 1 b) is 100 times the diameter of the analyte to be measured, preferably 50 times the diameter of the analyte, more preferably 25 times the diameter of the analyte. 6. The microfluidic device of any one of claims 1 -5, wherein the height of the fluid layer in the capillary fluid channel (4) is between 0.5 to 3 Î¼Î·Î·. 7. The microfluidic device of any one of claims 1 -6, wherein the hydrophobic surfaces of the first and second element (1 a, 1 b) are planar hydrophobic surfaces. 8. The microfluidic device of any one of claims 1 -7, wherein the first and 

 the second element (1 a, 1 b) are made of a material comprising glass and/or polymeric compounds. 9. The microfluidic device of any one of claims 1 -8, wherein the hydrophobic surface is a hydrophobic layer applied on the first and/or second element. 10. The microfluidic device of any one of claim 1 -9, wherein the hydrophilic pathway on the first and second element (1 a, 1 b) has a height of 0 to 3 nm above or below the hydrophobic surface. 11. The microfluidic device of any one of claims 1 -10, wherein the capture means may be attached to and/or be part of a 3D-structure. 12. The microfluidic device of any one of claims 1 -11 , wherein the first and second elements (1 a, 1 b) are spaced apart from each other by using spacing means. 13. The microfluidic device of any one of claims 1 -12, wherein the distance between the first and second element (1 a, 1 b) is adjustable by using spacing means of different heights. 14. The microfluidic device of claim 12 or 13, wherein the spacing means comprise a spacer, such as a maze, glue and/or an inert material. 15. The microfluidic device of any one of claims 1 -14, wherein the capture means comprise antibodies, receptors, enzymes, aptamers, molecularly imprinted polymers (MIPs). 16. The microfluidic device of any one of claims 1 -15, wherein the capillary fluid channel (4) has an inlet and an outlet; wherein the inlet is attached to a filter; and/or the outlet is attached to a driving force such as a vacuum, an absorbance pad, or a pump.
